scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Circular Dependency in Agent Outputs
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Circular dependency chain fully described — Bird assumes MJ defines propagation, MJ assumes Pippen defines async/background requirements, Pippen assumes Bird defines missing/invalid context handling, chain closes back on Bird. (2) Foundational gap clearly named — no agent owns missing/invalid tenant context handling. (3) All three specific gaps named: what constitutes valid context, what error is returned when missing/invalid, how async jobs handle context inheritance failures. (4) Escalation routed to Coach K with all gaps described and three resolution options. (5) What IS defined correctly summarized in high_confidence_areas (JWT source, TenantContext object, background job inheritance intent). (6) No invented error handling — explicitly poses 'Reject? 401? 403? Drop silently?' as open questions rather than answering them.

observations.text: Full circular chain traced with precise attribution of which assumption each agent made and which was unmet
Correctly identifies Bird as the agent whose assumption was NOT met, closing the loop
Recommendation (Option B) breaks the cycle by assigning explicit ownership rather than routing to Shaq
Low confidence areas map directly to the three foundational gaps required by the rubric
No brief produced for Shaq — correctly withheld synthesis until gaps are resolved
Confidence level of 35 appropriately reflects the severity of the unresolved circular dependency

agent_output_excerpt: {"escalations":[{"type":"circular_dependency","description":"The three agent outputs form a circular assumption chain: Bird assumes MJ will define tenant context propagation (MJ did). MJ assumes Pippen will define operational requirements for async/background propagation (Pippen did). Pippen assumes Bird has defined what happens when tenant context is missing or invalid (Bird did NOT). The chain closes back on Bird with an unmet assumption, meaning no agent owns the critical behavior for missing
